title: Language Apps Effectiveness 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Find the Best Language Learning App for Your Goals
description: Compare language learning apps for your level & goal. Free tool to find the best app—Duolingo, iTalki, Busuu & more.
intro: Learning a language requires hundreds of hours of consistent exposure. According to the FSI, for English speakers: French/Spanish ~750h, German ~900h, Arabic/Chinese/Japanese ~2200h. Key insight: consistency beats intensity. Find the right app for your learning goal. Our data is current through 2026 and regularly updated.
keyTakeaway: Tool: **2026 Language App Comparison**. Get personalized recommendations based on your current level and learning goal.
useCases: Evaluate language learning apps for your needs
Choose the right app for your level and goal
Find free or affordable language learning options
Understand language learning science and FSI data
explanation:
**Learning a language** is a sustained effort of hundreds of hours. Cognitive science (Ebbinghaus, spaced repetition) + immersion + active practice are the proven pillars validated by FSI and Cambridge.

## How it works

Select your current level (beginner, intermediate, or advanced) and your primary learning goal (vocabulary, grammar, conversation, or pronunciation). This tool recommends the best-matched language learning app for your needs and provides alternative options.

## Final notes

This is a reference guide based on FSI data and language learning science. For critical decisions, consult with a language learning specialist. Data is current through 2026 and regularly reviewed.

faq.a: Duolingo excels for beginner vocabulary and habit-building through gamification, but lacks real conversation practice. Best used as a supplement alongside speaking activities.

faq.q: How many hours per day should I study?
faq.a: 15–30 minutes daily beats occasional 2-hour cramming sessions. Consistency matters far more than intensity.

faq.q: Can I reach fluency using only apps?
faq.a: Apps are excellent for vocabulary and basics, but fluency requires real conversation. Combine apps with iTalki tutors, language exchange partners, or immersion.

faq.q: What's the best app for conversation practice?
faq.a: iTalki (live 1:1 tutors), HelloTalk (language exchange with native speakers), and Tandem offer the most authentic speaking practice.
Yes. All our calculators are free and require no signup.
Our suggestions are evidence-based using FSI data and cognitive science research. They're starting points—your learning style and discipline matter too.

faq.q: Which app is best for pronunciation?
faq.a: iTalki (speaking tutors), Speechling, Forvo (native speaker recordings), and Pimsleur all have strong pronunciation practice.

faq.q: Do these apps store my personal data?
faq.a: Most language apps store your progress in the cloud. Check their privacy policy. This calculator runs entirely in your browser—no data is collected or sent.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
